Landing Page Optimization Hypothesis

Bella & Bona B2B Landing Page Mockup

This mockup is designed as a conversion-focused alternative to the current hero section. The objective is to reduce cognitive overload, improve CTA clarity and shift the message from “food options” to a stronger workplace experience value proposition.

Optimized Hero Mockup

The redesigned hero uses one emotional headline, one dominant CTA, one human-centered image and immediate trust proof. It removes app store banners, floating meal labels and competing visual elements from the first screen.

Key UX Changes

1. Clearer value proposition

  • More emotional headline focused on workplace experience.
  • Less operational clutter above the fold.
  • Message aligned to HR and Office Manager pain points.

2. Single primary conversion action

  • One dominant CTA: “Book a Free Team Lunch”.
  • Removed app download banners from the hero section.
  • Reduced competing user actions in the first screen.

3. Human-centered visual

  • Image focuses on happy employees receiving lunch in the office.
  • No floating meal labels or visual distractions.
  • Positions Bella & Bona as an office experience, not just food delivery.

4. Stronger trust proof

  • “Loved by 200+ teams” appears close to the CTA.
  • Brand proof is visible before deeper page exploration.
  • Reduces perceived risk for first-time visitors from paid ads.

Conversion Hypothesis

Reduce friction Fewer competing elements should improve clarity and reduce bounce from cold paid traffic.
Increase CTA visibility A single, high-contrast CTA should improve click-through to the lead form.
Improve lead quality Stronger workplace positioning should attract companies with real employee benefit intent.
Recommended A/B test: current hero vs optimized hero, with primary metric set as visit → qualified lead conversion rate and secondary metrics including CTA click rate, form completion rate and lead-to-SQL rate.

Summary

The redesign is not meant as a cosmetic exercise. It is a conversion hypothesis: reduce above-the-fold clutter, strengthen emotional relevance and make the user journey clearer for high-intent B2B buyers arriving from Google or LinkedIn campaigns.
